faqs.a: Boundaries are the limits a person sets to protect their own wellbeing in relationships. They mark out what someone is and isn't comfortable with, how they want to be treated, and how much time and energy they can give. In plain terms, a boundary says this works for me and this doesn't.

No. Setting limits can feel uncomfortable, especially at first, but it isn't about pushing people away. Clear boundaries often make relationships healthier, because resentment and burnout have less room to build.

faqs.q: What is the difference between a boundary and controlling someone?
faqs.a: A boundary is about your own actions, what you'll accept and how you'll respond, not about forcing someone else to change. Controlling another person tries to dictate their behavior, while a boundary defines yours.
